segments.text: Thank you for calling. If this is a medical emergency, please hang up and dial 9-1-1 or head to the nearest ER
Bar espanol respond in Espanol. This is Sarah
I can help you with scheduling medical records billing and prescription refills. How can I help you?
Eduling could I get your first name and your last name?
Got it. Could you spell that for me just to be sure and your date of birth?
So that's November 13th, 1985, correct?
One moment while I look up your record by date of birth.
Let me check your appointments.
See you in our system.
Are you looking to book a new appointment, reschedule, cancel, or confirm an existing
one?
Book an appointment.
Before I get you scheduled, I'll need your insurance carrier and member ID.
Could you grab your card?
Great. What is your insurance carrier?
Big Nuh.
And what is your member ID?
Updating your insurance on file?
One moment. Got it. I've updated that on your record.
I see you were previously seen by Dr. Singh at our Mesa office.
Does the Mesa office still work for you or would you like a different office?
Yes, it works for me.
Perfect.
What kind of appointment are you looking for?
a regular follow-up, a diabetes check-in, or something specific like a thyroid biopsy?
Lab. Thanks for letting me know. Since lab appointments are handled a bit differently,
let me take a quick message and have our manager Bridgette call you back to help with
scheduling your labs. Could you please confirm your full name and the best callback number?
Got it. Taking down your message now. Your request is in our manager Bridgette will
call you back to help schedule your lab appointment at the MISA office. Is there
anything else I can help you with today? No. Thanks for calling. Have a great day.
